{{Quote|Luke, sometimes you almost make me miss Han Solo's boneheaded optimism!|Leia Organa|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 52">[[Star Wars (1977) 52|''Star Wars'' (1977) 52]]</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars 52: To Take The Tarkin''''' is the fifty-second issue in [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel's ''Star Wars'']] series of comics.

This issue appeared in a [[1981]] television commercial for [[Marvel Comics]].<ref name="Commercial">{{YouTube|video=jTZIEwnY_x8|text=Marvel Comics - "Summer Escape" (Commercial, 1981)|channel=@FuzzyMemoriesTV|name=The Museum of Classic Chicago Television}}</ref>

==Plot summary==
The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] along with the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] have infiltrated the Imperial [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] [[Tarkin (superweapon)|''Tarkin'']] with the intention of sabotaging it. While they are carrying out their plan, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] is arranging a confrontation with Luke, but a group of Imperial officers led by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Biel]], are planning to destroy the Sith Lord. The officers' plan fails, but Vader decides to focus on capturing the Rebels and deal with the insurgents later.

Having hampered the firing mechanism of the ''Tarkin'', the Rebels escape the station but are attacked by a group of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]], including one piloted by Vader. Fortunately for the Rebels, [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] had stowed aboard the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] and he pilots the freighter to the aid of the others. With Vader's fighter disabled, Biel attempts to fire the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} superlaser at the Rebels, hoping Vader would be caught in the blast. But the superweapon self-destructs at the time it fires, killing all on board.
